steps.title: Use slope for a change question
steps.content: The function $$F(x)=\frac{9}{5}(x-273.15)+32$$ is linear. Its slope is $\frac{9}{5}$, which means each $1$-kelvin increase changes Fahrenheit by $\frac{9}{5}$ degrees.

steps.title: Scale the increase
steps.content: For a $9.10$ kelvin increase, multiply by the slope: $$\Delta F=\frac{9}{5}(9.10)=16.38.$$ The constants $-273.15$ and $+32$ do not affect the change.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The result is correct. The larger choices come from evaluating the whole temperature conversion rather than finding the increase.
